pois.name: Bremer Roland
pois.description: An iconic 1404 limestone statue symbolizing civic liberty and independence, part of the UNESCO World Heritage site on the market square.

pois.name: Bremer Rathaus
pois.description: The city hall is one of the most important examples of Brick Gothic and Weser Renaissance architecture in Europe, dating back to 1405.

pois.name: St. Petri Dom Bremen
pois.description: A Protestant cathedral with twin towers dominating the skyline, featuring Romanesque and Gothic architectural elements and a lead cellar.

pois.name: Böttcherstraße
pois.description: A unique 100-meter long street built in the 1920s, famous for its Brick Expressionist architecture and the Glockenspiel House.

pois.name: Schnoor-Viertel
pois.description: Bremen's oldest district, characterized by narrow medieval alleys and well-preserved 15th to 18th-century houses.

pois.name: Schloss Schönebeck
pois.description: A 17th-century half-timbered manor house in Bremen-Vegesack, now serving as a local history museum for the surrounding region.

pois.name: Burg Blomendal
pois.description: A medieval moated castle built in 1354 by the Knights of Aumund, featuring well-preserved Renaissance ceiling paintings.

pois.name: U-Boot-Bunker Valentin
pois.description: A massive submarine pen built by forced labor during WWII, now serving as a poignant memorial and site of historical documentation.

pois.name: Simon-Loschen-Leuchtturm
pois.description: Built in 1854, this is the oldest functional lighthouse on the mainland of the German Bight and a symbol of Bremerhaven.

pois.name: Bürgermeister-Smidt-Gedächtniskirche
pois.description: A prominent Neo-Gothic church in Bremerhaven, serving as a landmark for sailors and a memorial to the city's founder.

pois.name: St. Martini Kirche
pois.description: A Gothic brick church located directly on the Weser, known for its restored interior and historically significant organ.

pois.name: Bremer Ratskeller
pois.description: One of the oldest wine cellars in Germany, housing the world's largest collection of German wines under the historic town hall.

pois.name: Beck's Brauerei
pois.description: The headquarters of the world-famous brewery, representing Bremen's long-standing tradition of beer production since 1873.

pois.name: Mühle am Wall
pois.description: A traditional Dutch windmill situated in the green belt of the former city fortifications, now a restaurant and city landmark.

pois.name: Schütting
pois.description: The historic guildhall of Bremen's merchants, located on the market square and showcasing an elegant Renaissance facade.

pois.name: Liebfrauenkirche
pois.description: The oldest parish church in the city, featuring distinct Romanesque foundations and impressive stained glass windows by Alfred Manessier.

pois.name: Dionysiuskirche Bremerhaven-Lehe
pois.description: A historic church in the Lehe district, reflecting the early ecclesiastical development of the region before the founding of the modern port.

pois.name: St. Johann Kirche
pois.description: A former Franciscan monastery church in the Schnoor district, built in the Brick Gothic style and now the main Catholic church of the center.

pois.name: Riensberger Friedhof
pois.description: A park-like cemetery known for its elaborate mausoleums and the graves of notable Bremen citizens, including historical garden design.

pois.name: Focke-Museum
pois.description: The state museum for art and cultural history, housing archaeological finds from the regional history of Bremen and the North Sea.

pois.name: Teerhofbrücke
pois.description: A pedestrian bridge connecting the old town with the Teerhof island, offering classic views of the city skyline and Weser river.

pois.name: Werdersee
pois.description: A large artificial lake and recreational area located in the Weser river plains, used for rowing and local leisure.

pois.name: Stadtwaldsee (Unisee)
pois.description: A popular lake for swimming and recreation situated near the University of Bremen and the municipal forest.

pois.name: Speicher XI
pois.description: A 400-meter long historic warehouse in the former port area, now housing the Harbor Museum and university facilities.

pois.name: Knoops Park
pois.description: A historic landscape park in St. Magnus overlooking the Lesum river, created for the merchant Ludwig Knoop.

pois.name: Schulschiff Deutschland
pois.description: The last fully rigged three-masted sailing ship under the German flag, permanently moored as a maritime monument in Vegesack.

pois.name: Vegesacker Hafen
pois.description: Founded in 1622 as Germany's first man-made harbor, it remains a central point for maritime tradition and old ships.

pois.name: Dionysiuskirche Wulsdorf
pois.description: A Romanesque fieldstone church in the Wulsdorf district, one of the oldest buildings in the Bremerhaven area.

pois.name: St. Stephani Kirche
pois.description: A historic church in the west of the old town, serving as Bremen's first 'cultural church' for modern events and exhibits.

pois.name: Alter Hafen Bremerhaven
pois.description: The site of the city's original 1827 harbor, now central to the maritime museum and tourism district.

pois.name: Neuer Hafen Bremerhaven
pois.description: A historic harbor basin adjacent to the dike, now hosting a variety of traditional sailing vessels and modern museum facilities.

pois.name: Bremer Stadtmusikanten
pois.description: The famous bronze statue of the donkey, dog, cat, and rooster from the Brothers Grimm fairy tale, a symbol of the city.

pois.name: Haus des Glockenspiels
pois.description: Located in Böttcherstraße, it features 30 Meissen porcelain bells that perform traditional sea shanties and folk songs.

pois.name: Roselius-Haus
pois.description: A late Gothic patrician house in Böttcherstraße, now a museum housing the collection of coffee merchant Ludwig Roselius.

pois.name: Weserpromenade Schlachte
pois.description: The historic quayside of Bremen, now a vibrant pedestrian zone with numerous restaurants, beer gardens, and traditional ships.

pois.name: Gasthof Zum Silbernen Schlüssel
pois.description: A traditional establishment reflecting the hospitality culture of old Bremen, situated near the historical center.

pois.name: Pingelturm
pois.description: A historic signal tower in Bremerhaven used to provide acoustic signals to ships entering the locks during fog.

pois.name: Geestemünder Wasserturm
pois.description: A prominent industrial monument in Bremerhaven, this water tower represents the 19th-century infrastructure expansion.

pois.name: Thieles Garten
pois.description: A unique private park in Bremerhaven-Leherheide featuring eccentric sculptures and a mix of botanical and artistic elements.

pois.name: Schloss Morgenstern
pois.description: A site of former fortifications and historical significance in Weddewarden, now associated with regional maritime history.

pois.name: St. Georg Kirche Seehausen
pois.description: A historic village church in the marshland district of Seehausen, representing the rural ecclesiastical history of the state.

pois.name: Vegesacker Wal-Kiefer
pois.description: Two massive jawbones of a bowhead whale erected as a gateway, commemorating Vegesack's history as a whaling port.

pois.name: Horner Mühle
pois.description: A well-preserved gallery windmill in the Horn-Lehe district, still used occasionally for demonstration milling.

pois.name: Union Brauerei Bremen
pois.description: A revitalized historic brewery in the Walle district, showcasing Bremen's industrial heritage and craft beer culture.

pois.name: Seemannskirche Bremerhaven
pois.description: A church specifically catering to the spiritual needs of sailors and port workers, located near the main harbor area.

pois.name: Paul-Ernst-Wilke-Haus
pois.description: The former studio of painter Paul Ernst Wilke, located directly on the dike in Bremerhaven as a tribute to local art.

pois.name: Waller Friedhof
pois.description: Bremen's largest cemetery, containing historic graves and serving as a large green space for the western part of the city.

pois.name: Burgwall Blumenthal
pois.description: The remains of an early medieval circular rampart fortification located in the Blumenthal district.

pois.name: St. Jakobi Kirche Seehausen
pois.description: A small historic church serving the rural community in the marshlands south of the Weser.

pois.name: Fischereihafen Bremerhaven
pois.description: The historic heart of the German deep-sea fishing industry, now a culinary and cultural destination.

pois.name: Olbers-Planetarium
pois.description: Named after the famous Bremen astronomer Heinrich Wilhelm Olbers, located on the navigation school site.

pois.name: Kleine Weser
pois.description: An old side arm of the Weser river forming a lake-like area in the middle of the city, popular for park strolls.

pois.name: Borgfelder Wümme-Wiesen
pois.description: A protected wetland area near Borgfeld, reflecting the traditional cultural landscape of the Wümme river delta.

pois.name: St. Cyriacus Mittelsbüren
pois.description: A small 13th-century church building that was relocated and integrated into the museum village site.

pois.name: Gasthaus Zur Ochtum
pois.description: A historic inn located near the Ochtum river, serving as a rest stop for travelers in the southern outskirts.

pois.name: Herz-Jesu-Kirche Bremerhaven
pois.description: A Neo-Gothic Catholic church in the Geestemünde district, known for its tall spire and brick detail.

pois.name: Aussichtsturm SAIL City
pois.description: An observation deck on the Atlantic Hotel Sail City, providing the highest panoramic views over Bremerhaven and the North Sea.

pois.name: Utkiek Vegesack
pois.description: A historic viewpoint at the mouth of the Lesum river, traditionally used to watch for incoming ships.

pois.name: Rhododendron-Park Bremen
pois.description: Home to one of the largest collections of rhododendrons and azaleas in the world, including a historic park landscape.

pois.name: Packhaus Schnoor 2
pois.description: A former warehouse in the Schnoor district, now one of the narrowest buildings used for retail and tourism.
